name: archetypes/statement
title: Statement
description: One claim at display size with an optional support line. The pause button of a deck: no figure, no list, just the sentence that matters.
useCases: landing the one sentence the audience must remember
a beat of emphasis between dense evidence slides

claim.description: One sentence that could carry the slide alone; cut until it does.

motion.notes: Claim and support enter with the core stagger; a statement never needs data-step.

icons.guidance: No icons — the sentence is the visual.

useWhen: one sentence deserves the whole slide
resetting attention between dense data slides
avoidWhen: the claim needs badges or a figure — that is an opener, use archetypes/title-hero
the sentence is really a number — use archetypes/big-stat and let the value speak

attributes.name: data-ls-archetype
attributes.description: Marks the slide for contract linting and deck scorecards.

usage: Write the claim as a verdict ("Ship the smallest change that proves the idea"), never a heading.
Use <em> for the two or three words doing the work; italicizing everything emphasizes nothing.
If you are tempted to add a third line, the content wants a different archetype.
